evals.prompt: I've staged some changes in this repo that add input validation to the login handler in internal/auth/. The repo's git log shows subjects like 'storage: retry uploads on transient errors' and 'auth: add token refresh'. Please commit the staged changes with an appropriate message.
evals.expected_output: A commit with subject like 'auth: validate login input' — scope-first, imperative, lowercase after colon, no Conventional Commits type prefix.
assertions: Scope is 'auth' (reused from existing history), not a newly invented near-duplicate
Subject does not start with a Conventional Commits type (feat, fix, chore, refactor, docs)
Description is in imperative mood with no trailing period
Message contains no emoji, em dashes, or Markdown formatting

evals.prompt: This is a brand-new project I just scaffolded (a Python CLI in src/todocli/ plus a README). Initialize git and make the first commit.
evals.expected_output: First commit uses a scoped subject such as 'todocli: scaffold initial CLI' or similar scope-first message, not 'Initial commit' with no scope and not 'feat: initial commit'.
assertions: No Conventional Commits type prefix is used
Message contains no emoji or em dashes

evals.prompt: Rewrite these commit messages to follow our project's commit style (CLAUDE.md says we use scoped commits): 1) 'feat(parser): added support for YAML anchors' 2) 'fix: bug' 3) 'Updated documentation and fixed typos in the API reference'
evals.expected_output: Rewrites like 'parser: add support for YAML anchors', 'docs: fix typos in API reference', with each subject scope-first, imperative, and specific. For 'fix: bug' the agent should recover specifics from the actual change or ask, not invent them.
assertions: Type prefixes (feat, fix) are removed rather than kept as scopes
For 'fix: bug', the agent asks for or looks up the underlying change instead of inventing a fictional description

evals.prompt: I refactored this service so rate limiting happens in one shared middleware instead of separately in each of the six HTTP handlers (search had no limiter at all before). Commit it with a message that explains the change for posterity. Make it a really good, detailed commit message!
evals.expected_output: A scoped subject like 'api: move rate limiting into shared middleware' with a short prose body explaining why, despite the prompt inviting an overwrought message.
assertions: Subject matches '<scope>: <description>' and stays under ~72 characters
Body is prose paragraphs, not a bullet list restating the diff ('Added X / Updated Y / Removed Z')
Body explains why (drifting per-handler limits, missing limiter on search), not just what
No emoji, em dashes, Markdown headings/bold, or 'Key changes:' style sections
No inflated adjectives (comprehensive, robust, seamless) or negative parallelism
